description:
# Why

`scripts/install-hooks.sh` symlinks `<git-common-dir>/hooks/pre-commit`
at `<git-common-dir>/../scripts/pre-commit` — the `scripts/` dir of a
presumed *main checkout* sitting beside `.git`. In the recommended
bare-hub deployment there is no main checkout: `scripts/` exists only
inside the ephemeral `.balls-worktrees/<id>/` worktrees. The symlink
target never exists, so `.git/hooks/pre-commit` is a permanently
dangling symlink — `test -x` fails on it, git treats the hook as
absent (`git hook run pre-commit` => "cannot find a hook named
pre-commit"), and every `git commit` from a worktree silently bypasses
the clippy / line-length / test / coverage gates.

# Scope

`install-hooks.sh` installs a self-contained stub into the shared hooks
dir instead of symlinking a fixed checkout. The stub resolves the
*committing* worktree at commit time (`git rev-parse --show-toplevel`)
and execs that worktree's own `scripts/pre-commit`. Worktrees with no
`scripts/pre-commit` (the `balls/tasks` state-branch worktree) commit
ungated, as before. Correct for bare hubs and ordinary clones alike;
a removed worktree never re-dangles the hook. Re-point the live
dangling `.git/hooks/pre-commit` by running the fixed installer.

# Relationship

Sibling of bl-1f38 (configurable pre-squash gate in `bl review`).
bl-1f38 Q5 names this dangling-symlink bug and defers it to a sibling
ball. This fixes the hook for manual worktree commits; bl-1f38 owns the
deeper "bl review squash bypasses the gates" problem.

# Out of scope

Whether install-hooks.sh should be retired once bl review gates — that
decision belongs to bl-1f38.
title: install-hooks.sh: pre-commit hook dangles in a bare hub
